title: Linkerd ServiceProfile
description: A Linkerd ServiceProfile is a Kubernetes Custom Resource that defines per-route traffic policy for a service. It enables per-route metrics, retries, timeouts, and traffic shaping based on request path and method matching.

name.description: The name of the ServiceProfile, which must match the DNS name of the service it describes (e.g., my-service.my-namespace.svc.cluster.local).

namespace.description: The Kubernetes namespace in which the ServiceProfile is created. Must match the namespace of the service.

labels.description: Arbitrary key-value labels attached to the resource.

annotations.description: Arbitrary key-value annotations attached to the resource.

ServiceProfileSpec.title: ServiceProfileSpec
ServiceProfileSpec.description: The specification for a Linkerd ServiceProfile, defining route matching rules and traffic policies.

routes.description: Ordered list of route definitions. Linkerd matches requests against routes in order and applies the policy of the first matching route.

RouteSpec.title: RouteSpec
RouteSpec.description: Defines a route with a name, condition for matching, and optional traffic policies such as timeout and retry.

name.description: Human-readable name for the route, used in metrics labels.

responseClasses.description: Classification rules that map responses to success or failure for metric purposes.

isRetryable.description: If true, failed requests on this route are eligible for automatic retry, subject to the retry budget.

timeout.description: Request timeout for this route in Go duration format (e.g., 250ms, 1s). Overrides any default timeout.

RequestMatch.title: RequestMatch
RequestMatch.description: Condition that determines whether an incoming request matches this route. Supports path regex, HTTP method, and boolean combinators.

pathRegex.description: A RE2-compatible regular expression matched against the request path.

all.description: All of the listed conditions must match (logical AND).

any.description: At least one of the listed conditions must match (logical OR).

not.description: The condition must NOT match (logical NOT).

ResponseClass.title: ResponseClass
ResponseClass.description: Maps a response matching a condition to a success or failure classification for metrics.

isFailure.description: If true, responses matching this condition are counted as failures. Defaults to false (success).

ResponseMatch.title: ResponseMatch
ResponseMatch.description: Condition for matching an HTTP response based on status code range.

status.description: Matches HTTP status codes within a numeric range.

RetryBudget.title: RetryBudget
RetryBudget.description: Controls the overall retry rate for a service to prevent retry storms. Defines what fraction of requests may be retried and a minimum number of retries per second.

retryRatio.description: The maximum ratio of retries to original requests. For example, 0.2 allows up to 20% of requests to be retried.

minRetriesPerSecond.description: Minimum number of retries allowed per second regardless of request volume, ensuring retries can occur at low load.

ttl.description: The duration over which the retry budget is calculated (e.g., 10s). Requests older than this are not counted.
